```{r fig.width=14/2.54, fig.height=14/2.54}
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 5),
        legend.key.size = unit(0.2, "cm"),
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(-7, 0.1, -5, 0.1))

# Recreate Mn plot with smaller fonts
p2_panel <- ggplot(ext_mn, aes(x = age, y = Value, fill = Fraction)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack", width = 18) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Mn_F2" = "#DDA0DD", "Mn_F3" = "#8B008B"),
                    labels = c("Mn F2", "Mn F3")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = age_limits, 
                     breaks = seq(ceiling(age_limits[1]/100)*100, floor(age_limits[2]/100)*100, by = 100),
                     labels = function(x) ifelse(x %% 200 == 0, as.character(x), ""),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(position = "left") +
  labs(x = "Age (cal yr BP)", y = "Mn redox fractions\n(µg/g)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = c(0.125, 0.42),
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        legend.title = element_blank(),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
        legend.key.size = unit(0.3, "cm"),
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(-5, 0.1, 0, 0.1))

# Recreate other plots with smaller fonts
# Recalculate scaling factors for panel plots
ngrip_y_range <- range(ext_ngrip$`18O_NGRIP`, na.rm = TRUE)
ngrip_y_limits <- c(ngrip_y_range[1] - 0.5, ngrip_y_range[2] + 0.5)

july_insol_range <- range(ext_insol_july$insol_July, na.rm = TRUE)
jan_insol_range <- range(ext_insol_jan$insol_Jan, na.rm = TRUE)
ngrip_panel_range <- range(ext_ngrip$`18O_NGRIP`, na.rm = TRUE)
scaling_factor_july_insol <- (ngrip_panel_range[2] - ngrip_panel_range[1]) / (july_insol_range[2] - july_insol_range[1])
offset_july_insol <- ngrip_panel_range[1] - july_insol_range[1] * scaling_factor_july_insol
scaling_factor_jan_insol <- (ngrip_panel_range[2] - ngrip_panel_range[1]) / (jan_insol_range[2] - jan_insol_range[1])
offset_jan_insol <- ngrip_panel_range[1] - jan_insol_range[1] * scaling_factor_jan_insol

# Prepare annotation text for insolation value ranges
july_insol_min <- round(min(ext_insol_july$insol_July, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
july_insol_max <- round(max(ext_insol_july$insol_July, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
jan_insol_min <- round(min(ext_insol_jan$insol_Jan, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
jan_insol_max <- round(max(ext_insol_jan$insol_Jan, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
insol_annotation <- paste0("Summer (red): ", july_insol_min, "-", july_insol_max, " W/m²\nWinter (blue): ", jan_insol_min, "-", jan_insol_max, " W/m²")

p4_panel <- ggplot() +
  # NGRIP raw and smoothed
  geom_line(data = ext_ngrip_raw, aes(x = age, y = `18O_NGRIP`), 
            color = "#2F4F4F", linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 0.1) +
  geom_line(data = ext_ngrip, aes(x = age, y = `18O_NGRIP`), 
            color = "#2F4F4F", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.4) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = age_limits, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "δ18O \n NGRIP(‰)",
    limits = ngrip_y_limits
  ) +
  labs(x = "") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y.left = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold", color = "#2F4F4F"),
        axis.text.y.left = element_text(color = "#2F4F4F", size = 7),
        axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(1, 0.1, -5, 0.1))


# p4_insol_panel: Dedicated insolation panel with both summer (left) and winter (right) axes
# p4_5_panel: Gerzensee July temperature (right axis only)
p4_5_panel <- ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = ext_julyT, aes(x = age, y = JulyT_Ger * scaling_factor_temp + offset_temp), 
            color = "#FF6347", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = age_limits, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    position = "right",
    labels = function(x) round((x - offset_temp) / scaling_factor_temp, 1)
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "July T \n Gerzensee(°C)") +
  theme_classic() +  
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y.right = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold", color = "#FF6347"),
        axis.text.y.right = element_text(color = "#FF6347"),
        axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(-10, 0.1, -15, 0.1))

# p4_6_panel: Heiri2015 Temperature panel version
p4_6_panel <- ggplot() +
  # Heiri confidence interval (ribbon)
  geom_ribbon(data = heiri_for_plot_panel, aes(x = age, ymin = T_neg_scaled, ymax = T_pos_scaled),
              fill = "#DC143C", alpha = 0.15) +
  # Heiri2015 as main comparison (full line)
  geom_line(data = heiri_for_plot_panel, aes(x = age, y = JulyT_scaled), 
            color = "#DC143C", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.9, linetype = "solid") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = age_limits, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    position = "left",
    labels = function(x) round((x - offset_heiri) / scaling_factor_heiri, 1)
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "July T\n stack (°C)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y.left = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold", color = "#DC143C"),
        axis.text.y.left = element_text(color = "#DC143C", size = 7),
        axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(-10, 0.1, -15, 0.1))

# p4_7_panel: GDGT Temperature panel version with interpolated data
p4_7_panel <- ggplot() +
  # Raw GDGT data (thin, light)
  geom_line(data = ext_julyT_gdgt, aes(x = age, y = JulyT_GDGT * scaling_factor_gdgt + offset_gdgt), 
            color = "#228B22", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.5, linetype = "solid") +
  # Interpolated GDGT as main comparison (darker, dotted)
  geom_line(data = ext_julyT_gdgt_interp, aes(x = age, y = JulyT_GDGT_interp * scaling_factor_gdgt + offset_gdgt), 
            color = "#228B22", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.9, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = age_limits, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    position = "right",
    labels = function(x) round((x - offset_gdgt) / scaling_factor_gdgt, 1)
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "GDGT Temp.\n(°C)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y.right = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold", color = "#228B22"),
        axis.text.y.right = element_text(color = "#228B22", size = 7),
        axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(-10, 0.1, -15, 0.1))

p6_panel <- ggplot() +
  # Smoothed data - before hiatus
  geom_line(data = hsi_total_chl_before, aes(x = age, y = Total_Chl),
            color = "#228B22", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  # Smoothed data - after hiatus
  geom_line(data = hsi_total_chl_after, aes(x = age, y = Total_Chl),
            color = "#228B22", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  # Raw data - before hiatus
  geom_line(data = hsi_total_chl_raw_before, aes(x = age, y = Total_Chl),
            color = "#228B22", linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 1) +
  # Raw data - after hiatus
  geom_line(data = hsi_total_chl_raw_after, aes(x = age, y = Total_Chl),
            color = "#228B22", linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = age_limits, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(position = "left") +
  geom_hline(yintercept=2.63, color="forestgreen", linetype = 1, linewidth=0.2, alpha = 0.9) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Total Chlorophylls\n(µg/g)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold", color = "#228B22"),
        axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(-10, 0.1, -10, 0.1))

p5_panel <- ggplot() +
  # Real data (young) - solid fill with standard alpha
  geom_area(data = betula_real_young, aes(x = age, y = BetulaHZM), 
            fill = "#FFA500", alpha = 0.3) +
  # Real data (old) - solid fill with standard alpha
  geom_area(data = betula_real_old, aes(x = age, y = BetulaHZM), 
            fill = "#FFA500", alpha = 0.6) +
  # Modeled data - patterned fill with diagonal lines
  ggpattern::geom_area_pattern(data = betula_modeled, aes(x = age, y = BetulaHZM),
            fill = "#FFA500", alpha = 0.1,
            pattern = "stripe",
            pattern_fill = "#FF8C00",
            pattern_colour = "#FFA500",
            pattern_density = 0.1,
            pattern_spacing = 0.05,
            pattern_alpha = 0.3,
            pattern_angle = 45) +
  # Solid line for real data
  geom_line(data = betula_real_young, aes(x = age, y = BetulaHZM), 
            color = "#FF8C00", linewidth = 0.35) +
  geom_line(data = betula_real_old, aes(x = age, y = BetulaHZM), 
            color = "#FF8C00", linewidth = 0.35) +
  # Dashed line for modeled data
  geom_line(data = betula_modeled, aes(x = age, y = BetulaHZM), 
            color = "#FF8C00", linewidth = 0.35, linetype = "dashed") +
  # Add "modelled" text in the middle of the modeled section
  annotate("text", x = mean(c(12700, 13258)), y = 7, 
           label = "modelled", size = 2, fontface = "italic", color = "#8B4500") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = age_limits, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(position = "right", limits = c(0, 36)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Betula HZM\n(%)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold", color = "#FF8C00"),
        axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(-10, 0.1, -10, 0.1))

p1_panel <- ggplot() +
  # Smoothed data - before hiatus
  geom_line(data = hsi_bphe_before, aes(x = age, y = Bacteriopheophytin_a),
            color = "#9370DB", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  # Smoothed data - after hiatus
  geom_line(data = hsi_bphe_after, aes(x = age, y = Bacteriopheophytin_a),
            color = "#9370DB", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  # Raw data - before hiatus
  geom_line(data = hsi_bphe_raw_before, aes(x = age, y = Bacteriopheophytin_a),
            color = "#9370DB", linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 1) +
  # Raw data - after hiatus
  geom_line(data = hsi_bphe_raw_after, aes(x = age, y = Bacteriopheophytin_a),
            color = "#9370DB", linewidth = 0.35, alpha = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = age_limits, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(position = "left") +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0.48, color="purple", linetype = 1, linewidth=0.25, alpha = 0.9) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Bacteriopheophytin a\n(µg/g)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold", color = "#9370DB"),
        axis.line.x = element_blank(),
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(-5, 0.1, -10, 0.1))

# Combine all plots
multiplot_panel <- plot_grid(p4_panel, p4_5_panel, p4_6_panel, p4_7_panel, p6_panel, p4_8, p5_panel, p1_panel, p_pig_norm_panel, p2_panel,
                          ncol = 1,
                          align = "v",
                          axis = "lr",
                          rel_heights = c(1.2, 0.4, 0.4, 0.4, 1, 0.7, 0.7, 0.9, 0.6, 1.3),
                          hjust = 0,
                          vjust = 0,
                          greedy = TRUE)

# Add overlays with adjusted positioning and font sizes
plot_left_panel <- 0.105 
plot_right_panel <- 0.9
plot_width_panel <- plot_right_panel - plot_left_panel

x_norm_boundaries_panel <- plot_left_panel + pmax(0, pmin(1, (climate_boundaries_interior - age_limits[1]) / (age_limits[2] - age_limits[1]))) * plot_width_panel
x_norm_start_panel <- plot_left_panel + pmax(0, pmin(1, (climate_periods$start - age_limits[1]) / (age_limits[2] - age_limits[1]))) * plot_width_panel
x_norm_end_panel <- plot_left_panel + pmax(0, pmin(1, (climate_periods$end - age_limits[1]) / (age_limits[2] - age_limits[1]))) * plot_width_panel

hsi_nodata_norm_start_panel <- plot_left_panel + pmax(0, pmin(1, (hsi_nodata_start - age_limits[1]) / (age_limits[2] - age_limits[1]))) * plot_width_panel
hsi_nodata_norm_end_panel <- plot_left_panel + pmax(0, pmin(1, (hsi_nodata_end - age_limits[1]) / (age_limits[2] - age_limits[1]))) * plot_width_panel

total_height_panel <- 1.2 + 0.45 + 1 + 0.7 + 0.7 + 0.9 + 1.0 + 1.3
top_two_plots_panel <- 1.2 + 0.45 + 0.7
hiatus_ymax_panel <- 1 - (top_two_plots_panel / total_height_panel) * 0.9

multiplot_panel_with_lines <- ggdraw(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), clip = "on") +
  # Add hiatus rectangle
  ggpattern::geom_rect_pattern(aes(xmin = hsi_nodata_norm_start_panel, xmax = hsi_nodata_norm_end_panel,
                                    ymin = 0.1, ymax = hiatus_ymax_panel),
                               fill = "gray80", alpha = 0.1,
                               pattern = "stripe", pattern_fill = "gray60",
                               pattern_color = "gray60", pattern_density = 0.3,
                               pattern_spacing = 0.01, pattern_angle = 45, 
                               pattern_alpha = 0.1,
                               color = "gray50", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0) +
  draw_plot(multiplot_panel, x = 0, y = 0, width = 1, height = 1) +
  # Add vertical dashed lines
  geom_segment(data = data.frame(x = x_norm_boundaries_panel),
               aes(x = x, xend = x, y = 0.1, yend = 1),
               linetype = "dashed", color = "gray30", linewidth = 0.3) +
  # Add text labels with smaller fonts
  geom_text(aes(x = (x_norm_start_panel[1] + x_norm_end_panel[1])/2, y = 1.02),
            label = "Pleniglacial", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(x = (x_norm_start_panel[2] + x_norm_end_panel[2])/2, y = 1.02),
            label = "Bølling", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(x = (x_norm_start_panel[3] + x_norm_end_panel[3])/2, y = 1.02),
            label = "Allerød", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(x = (x_norm_start_panel[4] + x_norm_end_panel[4])/2, y = 1.02),
            label = "Younger Dryas", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  # Add hiatus label
  geom_text(aes(x = (hsi_nodata_norm_start_panel + hsi_nodata_norm_end_panel)/2, y = 0.5),
            label = "Hiatus", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold", color = "gray30") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1.05), clip = "off")

print(multiplot_panel_with_lines)

# Save panel-sized figure with transparent background
ggsave("../figs/discussion_multiplot_v2_with_pigments_panel.png", multiplot_panel_with_lines,
       width = 14, height = 9.9, units = "cm", dpi = 300, bg = "transparent")

cat("Panel A figure saved: ../figs/discussion_multiplot_v2_with_pigments_panel.png\n")
cat("Dimensions: 14 × 9.9 cm (2/3 A4 width × 1/3 A4 height) at 300 DPI\n")

# Save as SVG for vector graphics
ggsave("../figs/FigDiscussion.svg",
       plot = multiplot_panel_with_lines,
       width = 14, height = 12.5, units = "cm", dpi = 220)
```

# Export Pinus + Betula Combined Panel Figure

```{r}
# Create combined Pinus + Betula figure showing modelled extension
pinus_betula_combined <- plot_grid(p4_8, p5_panel,
                                    ncol = 1,
                                    align = "v",
                                    axis = "lr",
                                    rel_heights = c(0.7, 0.7),
                                    hjust = 0,
                                    vjust = 0,
                                    greedy = TRUE)

# Add overlays
plot_left_panel_pb <- 0.105 
plot_right_panel_pb <- 0.9
plot_width_panel_pb <- plot_right_panel_pb - plot_left_panel_pb

hsi_nodata_norm_start_panel_pb <- plot_left_panel_pb + pmax(0, pmin(1, (hsi_nodata_start - age_limits[1]) / (age_limits[2] - age_limits[1]))) * plot_width_panel_pb
hsi_nodata_norm_end_panel_pb <- plot_left_panel_pb + pmax(0, pmin(1, (hsi_nodata_end - age_limits[1]) / (age_limits[2] - age_limits[1]))) * plot_width_panel_pb

x_norm_boundaries_panel_pb <- plot_left_panel_pb + pmax(0, pmin(1, (climate_boundaries_interior - age_limits[1]) / (age_limits[2] - age_limits[1]))) * plot_width_panel_pb
x_norm_start_panel_pb <- plot_left_panel_pb + pmax(0, pmin(1, (climate_periods$start - age_limits[1]) / (age_limits[2] - age_limits[1]))) * plot_width_panel_pb
x_norm_end_panel_pb <- plot_left_panel_pb + pmax(0, pmin(1, (climate_periods$end - age_limits[1]) / (age_limits[2] - age_limits[1]))) * plot_width_panel_pb

pinus_betula_with_overlays <- ggdraw(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), clip = "on") +
  # Add hiatus rectangle
  ggpattern::geom_rect_pattern(aes(xmin = hsi_nodata_norm_start_panel_pb, xmax = hsi_nodata_norm_end_panel_pb,
                                    ymin = 0.1, ymax = 1),
                               fill = "gray80", alpha = 0.1,
                               pattern = "stripe", pattern_fill = "gray60",
                               pattern_color = "gray60", pattern_density = 0.3,
                               pattern_spacing = 0.01, pattern_angle = 45, 
                               pattern_alpha = 0.1,
                               color = "gray50", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0) +
  draw_plot(pinus_betula_combined, x = 0, y = 0, width = 1, height = 1) +
  # Add vertical dashed lines
  geom_segment(data = data.frame(x = x_norm_boundaries_panel_pb),
               aes(x = x, xend = x, y = 0.1, yend = 1),
               linetype = "dashed", color = "gray30", linewidth = 0.3) +
  # Add text labels
  geom_text(aes(x = (x_norm_start_panel_pb[1] + x_norm_end_panel_pb[1])/2, y = 1.02),
            label = "Pleniglacial", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(x = (x_norm_start_panel_pb[2] + x_norm_end_panel_pb[2])/2, y = 1.02),
            label = "Bølling", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(x = (x_norm_start_panel_pb[3] + x_norm_end_panel_pb[3])/2, y = 1.02),
            label = "Allerød", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(aes(x = (x_norm_start_panel_pb[4] + x_norm_end_panel_pb[4])/2, y = 1.02),
            label = "Younger Dryas", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold") +
  # Add hiatus label
  geom_text(aes(x = (hsi_nodata_norm_start_panel_pb + hsi_nodata_norm_end_panel_pb)/2, y = 0.5),
            label = "Hiatus", size = 2.5, fontface = "bold", color = "gray30") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1.05), clip = "off")

print(pinus_betula_with_overlays)

# Save Pinus + Betula figure as PNG
ggsave("../figs/Pinus_Betula_pollen_combined.png", pinus_betula_with_overlays,
       width = 14, height = 6, units = "cm", dpi = 300, bg = "transparent")

cat("Pinus + Betula combined figure saved: ../figs/Pinus_Betula_pollen_combined.png\n")
cat("Dimensions: 14 × 6 cm at 300 DPI\n")

# Save as SVG for vector graphics
ggsave("../figs/Pinus_Betula_pollen_combined.svg",
       plot = pinus_betula_with_overlays,
       width = 14, height = 6, units = "cm", dpi = 220)

cat("Vector version saved: ../figs/Pinus_Betula_pollen_combined.svg\n")
```
